entry.title: Re: HANA Query to track how many sales documents each use...
entry.content:
Added the Date prompt now, was getting caught out by the different case in CreateDate and createDate! 

Completed query below in case anyone else wants it:

SELECT "U_NAME" "User Name",
sum(case when "DocType" = 'SO' then "Count" else 0 end) as "Sales Order",
sum(case when "DocType" = 'PL' then "Count" else 0 end) as "Pick List",
sum(case when "DocType" = 'DN' then "Count" else 0 end) as "Delivery Note",
sum(case when "DocType" = 'AR' then "Count" else 0 end) as "AR Invoice",
sum(case when "DocType" = 'CN' then "Count" else 0 end) as "Credit Note",
sum(case when "DocType" = 'PO' then "Count" else 0 end) as "Purchase Order",
sum(case when "DocType" = 'PD' then "Count" else 0 end) as "Goods Rec PO",
sum(case when "DocType" = 'PU' then "Count" else 0 end) as "AP Invoice",
sum(case when "DocType" = 'PC' then "Count" else 0 end) as "AP Credit Note",
sum(case when "DocType" = 'SC' then "Count" else 0 end) as "Service Call"


FROM
(
SELECT T1."U_NAME", 'SO' "DocType", COUNT(T2."DocNum") AS "Count" FROM OUSR T1 LEFT JOIN ORDR T2 ON T1."USERID" = T2."UserSign"
WHERE T2."CreateDate" >= [%0] AND T2."CreateDate" <= [%1]
GROUP BY T1."U_NAME"
Union
SELECT T1."U_NAME", 'PL' "DocType", COUNT(T2."AbsEntry") AS "Count" FROM OUSR T1 LEFT JOIN OPKL T2 ON T1."USERID" = T2."OwnerCode"
WHERE T2."CreateDate" >= [%0] AND T2."CreateDate" <= [%1]
GROUP BY T1."U_NAME"
Union
SELECT T1."U_NAME", 'DN' "DocType", COUNT(T2."DocNum") AS "Count" FROM OUSR T1 LEFT JOIN ODLN T2 ON T1."USERID" = T2."UserSign"
WHERE T2."CreateDate" >= [%0] AND T2."CreateDate" <= [%1]
GROUP BY T1."U_NAME"
Union
SELECT T1."U_NAME", 'AR' "DocType", COUNT(T2."DocNum") AS "Count" FROM OUSR T1 LEFT JOIN OINV T2 ON T1."USERID" = T2."UserSign"
WHERE T2."CreateDate" >= [%0] AND T2."CreateDate" <= [%1]
GROUP BY T1."U_NAME"
Union
SELECT T1."U_NAME", 'CN' "DocType", COUNT(T2."DocNum") AS "Count" FROM OUSR T1 LEFT JOIN ORIN T2 ON T1."USERID" = T2."UserSign"
WHERE T2."CreateDate" >= [%0] AND T2."CreateDate" <= [%1]
GROUP BY T1."U_NAME"
Union
SELECT T1."U_NAME", 'PO' "DocType", COUNT(T2."DocNum") AS "Count" FROM OUSR T1 LEFT JOIN OPOR T2 ON T1."USERID" = T2."UserSign"
WHERE T2."CreateDate" >= [%0] AND T2."CreateDate" <= [%1]
GROUP BY T1."U_NAME"
Union
SELECT T1."U_NAME", 'PD' "DocType", COUNT(T2."DocNum") AS "Count" FROM OUSR T1 LEFT JOIN ORPC T2 ON T1."USERID" = T2."UserSign"
WHERE T2."CreateDate" >= [%0] AND T2."CreateDate" <= [%1]
GROUP BY T1."U_NAME"
Union
SELECT T1."U_NAME", 'PU' "DocType", COUNT(T2."DocNum") AS "Count" FROM OUSR T1 LEFT JOIN OPCH T2 ON T1."USERID" = T2."UserSign"
WHERE T2."CreateDate" >= [%0] AND T2."CreateDate" <= [%1]
GROUP BY T1."U_NAME"
Union
SELECT T1."U_NAME", 'PC' "DocType", COUNT(T2."DocNum") AS "Count" FROM OUSR T1 LEFT JOIN ORPC T2 ON T1."USERID" = T2."UserSign"
WHERE T2."CreateDate" >= [%0] AND T2."CreateDate" <= [%1]
GROUP BY T1."U_NAME"
Union
SELECT T1."U_NAME", 'SC' "DocType", COUNT(T2."DocNum") AS "Count" FROM OUSR T1 LEFT JOIN OSCL T2 ON T1."USERID" = T2."assignee"
WHERE T2."createDate" >= [%0] AND T2."createDate" <= [%1]
GROUP BY T1."U_NAME"
)

GROUP BY "U_NAME"
HAVING
sum(case when "DocType" = 'SO' then "Count" else 0 end)>0 or
sum(case when "DocType" = 'PL' then "Count" else 0 end)>0 or
sum(case when "DocType" = 'DN' then "Count" else 0 end)>0 or
sum(case when "DocType" = 'AR' then "Count" else 0 end)>0 or
sum(case when "DocType" = 'CN' then "Count" else 0 end)>0 or
sum(case when "DocType" = 'PO' then "Count" else 0 end)>0 or
sum(case when "DocType" = 'PU' then "Count" else 0 end)>0 or
sum(case when "DocType" = 'PC' then "Count" else 0 end)>0 or
sum(case when "DocType" = 'SC' then "Count" else 0 end)>0

ORDER BY "U_NAME"

entry.title: Step into the Future at the International Conference of Energy and Utilities!
entry.content:
What if you could step into the shoes of your customer or even an electron? What if you could time travel to see the results of your distributed energy resources transformation?

Get ready for an exclusive preview of these answers at this year's International Conference of Energy and Utilities in Rotterdam. We are thrilled to introduce one of our signature assets for an unforgettable experience: the SAP Pop Up Campus.

This unique mobile venue has been transformed into an immersive environment designed to captivate and inspire. The Pop Up Campus offers a new and immersive experience, surrounding you with a compelling storyline that brings the future to life.

Discover the Future of Customer Engagement and Technology

 

 

 

 

 

Watch the video

Join us at our dedicated conference track to explore the future of customer engagement, asset management, and cutting-edge technology. Step into an immersive environment featuring a massive 180° LED screen that brings complex concepts and solutions to life with stunning clarity and impact. Engage with the latest innovations, connect with industry leaders, and exchange ideas in a setting that fosters collaboration, learning, and inspiration.

A Dynamic Blend of Networking and Learning

The Pop Up Campus offers a dynamic blend of networking, knowledge sharing, and experiential learning, creating an unparalleled opportunity to engage with the latest advancements in a truly interactive format. Here's a sneak peek at our presentation menu:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • AI for Utilities: Experience the future of AI capabilities in SAP solutions for utilities.
  • A Day in the Life of a Water Drop / Electron: Dive deep into the benefits that asset management provides at various stages of a life cycle as a water drop or electron.
  • Customer-Centric Services in SAP CX for Utilities: Gain a new perspective on how customer-centric services and interactions can be built with SAP Customer Experience solutions.
  • The Future of Distributed Energy Generation: Take a look into S.ENERGY in 2028 and discover how they will serve their customers after a successful SAP S/4HANA Utilities and DER platform implementation.

You can find these presentations and more on our agenda!

Ready to Revolutionise Your Business?

Don't miss out on this opportunity to revolutionise your business. Due to limited space, these presentations require pre-booking. Reservations can be made via the Event App, available only after registration. Secure your ticket as soon as registration opens and take the first step towards the next era of business innovation.

Join us and be part of the future from April 9th to 10th!

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Replication Flow - Projection Functions
entry.content:
Hi All

Where can I see a list of what "Functions" are available when creating a projection mapping rule, and how to use the functions?

I am trying to create a mapping rule in the Replication Flow whereby a target column gets the first 6 characters of a particular source column.

So when creating a Replication Flow, in the Projection section of the target object I have clicked on "Add".

On the "Mapping" page, I have added a new column (string 6) and ticked the box under "Functions / Constants".  In the text box I was then expecting to be able to type LEFT(field1,6) so that I can map the first 6 characters of field 1 to the new field.

But on clicking deploy, I get an error message suggesting the length of the value is 14 when expecting only 6.  I have worked out that the system is counting the length of the text "LEFT(field1,6)" - ie. the system is seeing my mapping rule as a Constant and not a Function.

How do I get the mapping rule to use a Function?  Or am I trying to do something that's not possible and the "Functions / Constants" description in the mapping section is misleading?

I realise I could create another table that includes the new field and update accordingly in a transformation flow, but I was hoping to do such mappings at load time to the replication flow target to avoid duplicating data.

entry.title: Re: Making SAP Business One Web Client accessible externally using nginx as a reverse proxy
entry.content:
Hi Pradeep

You are right, the configuration steps for Nginx on Windows are pretty much the same except the change you highlighted in your comment.
The “400 Bad Request – Request Header or Cookie Too Large” error in nginx typically happens when the HTTP request headers (including cookies) exceed the default size limits set by nginx. It could be that the authentication method in SQL differs slighly from the one in HANA resulting in a different request header, but in order to investigate further, I suggest to report an incident to Support with relevant Nginx Logs. Plz let me know once an incident is created so I will try to track it down from my end to evaluate if any needed changes are required on our side for the Nginx configuration templates.

Best Rgs
Guy

entry.title: SAP CCO Manager installation issue
entry.content:
Hello,

We have an issue on installing SAP CCO Manager.

We have installed B1Integration Framework and we must install CCO Manager on the same server. Tomcat don't want to install ccos. The ccos.war file is extract but we can't access to http://<server>:8080/ccos it say in french :

État HTTP 404 – Non trouvé

Type Rapport d'état

message La ressource demandée [/ccos] n'est pas disponible

description La ressource demandée n'est pas disponible.

Apache Tomcat/10.1.15

In catalina logs : 
18-Feb-2025 15:05:29.806 AVERTISSEMENT [main] org.apache.catalina.webresources.Cache.getResource Incapable d'ajouter la ressource située [/WEB-INF/classes/reports/SalesReport.json] au cache de l'application web [/ccos] parce qu'il n'y avait pas assez d'espace libre disponible après l'éviction des entrées de cache expirées - envisagez d'augmenter la taille maximale du cache
18-Feb-2025 15:05:43.609 GRAVE [main] org.apache.catalina.core.StandardContext.startInternal Un ou plusieurs écouteurs n'ont pas pu démarrer, les détails sont dans le fichier de log du conteneur
18-Feb-2025 15:05:43.616 GRAVE [main] org.apache.catalina.core.StandardContext.startInternal Erreur de démarrage du contexte [/ccos] suite aux erreurs précédentes

I have change context.xml to change 

<Resources cacheMaxSize="51200" />

Nothing appened, so i changed for

<Resources cachingAllowed="false" />

But nothing appened again.

Can someone help please ?

entry.title: Introducing Enablement Offerings and Trainings for SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
In this blog post, I'll introduce you to all the Enablement Offerings and Trainings available for SAP Commerce Cloud.
If you prefer a live presentation with a Q&A session, mark your calendars for our webcast on March 12th. You can register here, and a recording will be available afterwards:

What We Offer

As part of the Commerce team, also known as Education SAP Commerce Cloud under Product Learning CRM and Customer Experience of SAP Learning, we develop and manage training and enablement content for SAP Commerce Cloud. Our essential offerings include:
  • Instructor-Led Trainings (ILTs)
  • Guided E-Learning Journeys
  • Hands-on Practice Systems
  • Live Sessions
  • Bite-sized Video Learnings

Let's explore each offering in detail.

Instructor-Led Trainings (ILTs)

Our ILTs, led by authorized SAP instructors, provide real-time interaction for inquiries and feedback. In addition to covering essential theory explained by the instructors, these sessions include important hands-on exercises, allowing participants to apply and experience the knowledge they have gained.
In terms of delivery formats, we provide:
  • In-person Training: Traditional classroom style with experienced instructors.
  • Virtual Live Classroom (VLC): Online training via secured platforms like Zoom, maintaining the same quality and content as in-person sessions, while learners can attend the training directly from within the company or even home.
  • Hybrid Training: Combines in-person classroom sessions with simultaneous Virtual Live Classroom (VLC) broadcasts, providing flexibility for learners to choose their preferred mode of attendance.
In addition to the different ways of delivery, the ILTs can also be booked via:
  • Public Trainings: These sessions follow a predefined schedule accessible to learners worldwide. You can find the schedules directly on the individual training pages in the SAP training shop, typically listed right after the course information section.
  • Private Trainings: Tailored to meet your specific needs, you can request customized training sessions. Simply scroll to the bottom of the individual training page where you'll find the "Can't find a suitable date?" section offering two options:
    • Request a training date: Ideal for booking when you have 1-2 learners.
    • 3 to RUN training: Suitable for groups of 3 or more learners, allowing you to organize a dedicated session. Fill out the request form with the necessary details, and you'll be contacted for further arrangements.

Here are some of the popular ILTs that attract most learners:

  • C4H340: SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1
  • C4H341: SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2
  • C4H320: SAP Commerce Cloud Business User Training
  • C4H365: SAP Commerce Cloud Backoffice Framework Developer Training
  • C4H370: SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront Developer Training

Guided E-Learning Journeys

For those who cannot dedicate time to attend ILTs directly but want to learn SAP Commerce Cloud at their own pace, we offer several dedicated guided E-Learning journeys. These are perfect for building in-demand SAP development skills.
Access all SAP learning journeys through this link, or use filters to find specific SAP Commerce Cloud E-learning journeys, some most important/interesting ones are listed below:
 
For business users:
  • Understanding the Purpose of SAP Commerce Cloud, its Components, and User Roles (beginner level)
  • Managing Products in the Backoffice Product Cockpit (beginner level)
  • Creating and Configuring Web Content for SAP Commerce Cloud (beginner level, link forthcoming)
For technical users:
  • Explore the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud (beginner level)
  • Expand upon Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud (intermediate level)
  • Master the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud (advanced level)
For AI enthusiasts:
  • Utilizing SAP CX AI Toolkit for SAP Commerce Cloud (beginner level)
  • Enabling AI-Based Personalized Product Recommendation with Intelligent Selling Services for SAP Commerce Cloud (beginner level)
For SAP Commerce Cloud Integration with S/4HANA:
  • Discovering the Foundations of SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Integrations (beginner level)
  • Implement an Integration of SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Commerce Cloud (intermediate level)
Please note that all the E-learning journeys are categorized by proficiency levels:
  • Beginner level: Foundational concepts with no prior experience needed.
  • Intermediate level: Practical applications with moderate complexity.
  • Advanced level: In-depth topics for expert-level proficiency.
For many of these E-learning journeys, hands-on practice systems are also available, which I'll explain next.

Hands-on Practice Systems

Our practice systems are perfect for learners eager to gain hands-on experience with SAP Commerce Cloud but who cannot attend dedicated training sessions (or even after attending but want to redo the exercises). Each practice system includes:
  • A live, pre-configured SAP VM with a ready-to-run SAP Commerce Cloud Server.
  • Learning materials available on the VM for self-study, mirroring conventional training content:
  • Presentation slides in PDF format covering theoretical concepts (especially from ILTs).
  • Step-by-step exercise PDFs to guide your learning journey.
  • For technical training practice systems:
    • Pre-installed and configured Integrated Development Environment (IDE) such as Eclipse or VSCode.
    • Exercise source code and solutions.
You can view all SAP learning practice systems through this link. Use the "product" filter to focus on SAP Commerce Cloud practice systems, or access them directly here.
Specifically, many of the ILTs and guided E-learning journeys mentioned earlier have corresponding hands-on practice systems, such as:
  • For Regular Trainings:
    • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1
    • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2
    • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Business User Training (Formerly Hy200)
    • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud, Composable Storefront Developer Training
    • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Backoffice Framework Developer
  • For E-learning:
    • Hands-on Practice for Exploring the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud (beginner)
    • Hands-on Practice for Expanding upon SAP Commerce Cloud Technical Essentials (intermediate)
    • Hands-on Practice for Mastering the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud (advanced)

Live Sessions

Our live sessions are led by SAP-authorized instructors and provide opportunities for real-time questions and discussions. These sessions differ from ILTs in several ways:
  • Shorter Duration: Live sessions typically last 1-2 hours or are split into several short segments.
  • Focused Content: Each session hones in on a single module, component, or use case of a feature within SAP Commerce Cloud.
  • Up-to-date Information: Since they target specific topics, they are more frequently updated than courses covering multiple modules.
  • Accessibility: Free for SAP employees (with SAP Learning Hub internal edition) and customers/partners with a valid SAP Learning Hub subscription.
Access all the SAP live sessions directly through this link. If you are interested in specific SAP Commerce Cloud topics (all are here), you may find these selected live sessions valuable:
  • Discovering SAP Commerce Cloud: A 101 Guide to Core Features (beginner)
  • Identifying Scripting Support in SAP Commerce Cloud (beginner)
  • Introducing Backoffice Access Management in SAP Commerce Cloud (beginner)
  • Introducing Interceptors in SAP Commerce Cloud (beginner)
  • Understanding Hot Folders and Cloud Hot Folders in SAP Commerce Cloud (Intermediate)
  • Monitoring SAP Commerce Cloud with Dynatrace (recording) (intermediate)
  • Configuring Integration API Module in SAP Commerce Cloud (advanced)
  • Exploring Outbound Features of the Integration API Module in SAP Commerce Cloud (advanced)
  • Getting started on Composable Storefront Development (formerly known as Spartacus) (advanced)
  • Unleashing the Power of Composable Storefront SSR: Dive into Live Code and Demos (advanced)

Bite-sized Video Learnings

Similar to other SAP product lines, SAP Commerce Cloud video resources offer the following benefits:
  • Flexible Learning: Access resources anywhere and anytime, enabling global upskilling at your convenience.
  • Ease of Use: Short, focused videos highlight key features.
  • No Cost: Available to everyone complimentary.
View all available bite-sized videos through this link, then use the Product filter to focus on SAP Commerce Cloud content - or search directly through this link. You can also use the search function for specific content.
Here are a few selected videos learners may find interesting:
  • Using SAP CX AI Toolkit in SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Introducing Session Replay in Dynatrace for SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Connecting SmartEdit to SAP Commerce Cloud, Composable Storefront
  • Applying Dynamic Attributes in SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Log Monitoring with Cloud Logging System (CLS) in SAP Commerce Cloud

An Important Question: How to Find the Suitable Content to Learn?

Given the abundance of trainings and enablement content available, it can be challenging to determine which resources are most suitable for your learning needs. To help with this, we've created learning journeys for each SAP product, accessible here. Simply search "SAP Commerce Cloud," and you should see our learning journeys among the top results. Inside, you'll find pathways targeted at different roles:
  • Business Users
  • Consultants
  • Administrators 
  • Developers
Please note that these learning journeys may not contain every enablement offering or training mentioned in this blog post.
If you have additional questions about finding suitable content to learn, please reach out to our team at cx-training@sap.com. We’re happy to assist!

Last But Not Least

If you want to stay informed about future enablement offerings for SAP Commerce Cloud, be sure to subscribe this blog or me directly by clicking the "subscribe" button on the left.
Thank you for reading, and I look forward to seeing you next time!

entry.title: Re: GRC 12.0 Integration with S4HANA RISE (Private Cloud)
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Issue with SQL Query in SAP B1 HANA – Date Range Para...
entry.content:
Hi,

Just add table alias, then your query works

try this

SELECT
T0."DocEntry" AS "Document ID",
T0."DocNum" AS "Invoice Number",
T0."DocDate" AS "Document Date",
T0."CardCode" AS "Partner Code",
T0."CardName" AS "Partner Name",
T1."ItemCode" AS "Item Code",
T2."ItemName" AS "Item Name",
T1."AcctCode" AS "GL Account",
T1."OcrCode" AS "Cost Center",
T1."OcrCode2" AS "Division",
T1."OcrCode5" AS "Other Data",
T1."LineTotal" AS "Line Total Price"
FROM OPCH T0
JOIN PCH1 T1 ON T0."DocEntry" = T1."DocEntry"
LEFT JOIN OITM T2 ON T1."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode"
WHERE
T0."DocDate" BETWEEN '[%0]' AND '[%1]'
AND (T1."OcrCode" = '[%2]' OR '[%2]' IS NULL)
AND (T1."OcrCode" IS NOT NULL OR '[%3]' = 'Y')
ORDER BY T0."DocNum", T1."LineNum";

entry.title: Material Standard Cost Estimate (CO-PC-PCP)
entry.content:
Material cost estimation is a process of calculating the expected cost of manufacturing or procuring material. This is used to determine the standard cost of a product based on its components, production activities, and the overhead costs.

Cost estimation considers several factors such as raw material price, labor costs, machine costs, overhead costs, and subcontracting costs. It ensures accurate product costing, inventory valuation, and profitability analysis. This will be used to determine the pricing of the product and budgeting.

Integration with other module
Integration with other module

Costing materials with a quantity structure enables you to calculate the cost of goods manufactured and cost of goods sold for each product unit without reference to orders. A quantity structure typically consists of a bill of material (BOM) and a routing. But in the process industries, a recipe is used instead of a BOM. You can use the results of material cost estimates with a quantity structure to value materials at standard cost.

The principle of product cost estimation
The principle of product cost estimation

We can cost out individual materials, or multiple materials simultaneously in costing runs. Then, standard costs can be updated to the material master as the future standard price or current standard price.

A. Costing Structure
1. Costing Variant (T-Code: OKKN)
You can define how the cost estimate is calculated by customizing the cost control which contains costing type, valuation variant, and date control.


Maintain costing variant

  • Costing Type: Determines the purpose of the cost estimate (e.g., standard cost estimate, simulation, product cost collector, etc).
  • Valuation Variant: Determines whether standard price or moving average price is used. It also specifies how materials, activity types, and overheads are valued.
  • Quantity structure (BOM & Routing): BOM is to define raw materials and semi-finished products used as the component to produce a product. Routing is to define the operations, work centers, and activities for production. 

    B. Steps in Material Cost Estimate with Quantity Structure
    These are the end-to-end process flow from creating material to material price analysis. But in this article we will just focus on the material cost estimate calculation part.


    Material cost estimation flow in SAP

    1. Go to transaction code CK11N

    Here you need to fill the material and plant. For the costing data, choose the costing variant you want to use as you’ve been configuring it in T-Code OKKN before.


    Material cost estimate with quantity structure initial screen

    The dates need to be in the period of the cost center you maintain when maintaining the activity price at T-Code KP26.


    Material cost estimate with quantity structure initial screen (Dates)

    2. Make sure all cost structure error status is green. Usually there will be an error message if the calculation is not complete. For example you didn’t fill the cost center planning in KP26. Or you enter the wrong period of pricing so the cost will not be calculated. 

    On the cost component view, you can see the COGS and COGM has been calculated based on production routing, bill of material, and other expenses.


    Material cost estimate with quantity structure

    3. After you get the cost estimate, go to transaction code CK24 to mark and release the standard price. Insert the posting period and make sure the quantity that cost estimate updated successfully is not 0.


    Mark standard price

    Then, you can check the price at material master (T-Code: MM02/MM03 → Costing 2).
    The marked price will appear as the future price, but you can also still see the current price before we run the material cost estimate.


    Material master: Costing 2 Future Price

    If you are sure with the standard price calculation, you can go back to CK24 to release the standard price.


    Release standard price

    You can check back on the material master, the material cost estimate is moved to the current price after we release it.


    Material master: Costing 2 Current Price

     

    So that's basically how you calculate the standard cost from material cost estimates. Feel free to discuss more if you have any other insights or questions! Hope this helps to understand the basics of material cost estimation. 

     

    A lifetime learner,
    Dafina

entry.title: Goods Movements: Transfer Posting using MIGO
entry.content:
Transfer Posting is one of the methods used to move materials in Goods Movements. Within Goods Movements, several transaction types are available, and in SAP S/4HANA, these are streamlined into a single transaction: MIGO.

Transfer posting involves directly recording material movements in the system. It is important to note that transfer posting is not mutually exclusive with physical stock transfers. Both processes can be utilized depending on the business requirements.

Transfer postings and physical stock transfers can be categorized based on the following criteria:

    • Within one plant or across multiple plants
    • With or without delivery
    • One-step or two-step process
    • Intracompany or intercompany transfers
    • Stock transfer orders

This categorization helps organizations choose the most appropriate method for managing material movements efficiently.

This diagram shows the scope of the categorization. Transfer posting is denoted by the yellow color.

 

Diagram for Transfor Posting and Physical Stock Transfer

 

 

 

Transfer Posting Stock-to-Stock Within the Same Plant

*Note: it requires initial stock

TP Stock-to-Stock One Plant

 

 

 

 

 

e.g Transfer Posting Unrestricted Stock to Quality Inspection

 

 

SAP Demonstration

Initial stock: 100 Unrestricted Stock

 

 

 

Transfer Posting Process

Final Stock

Transfer Posting Material-to-Material

 

 

Key points from this type of transfer posting:

  • Can not be from reservation
  • From unrestricted use
  • Single step
  • Single UoM

 

Consignment-to-Stock

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

_

One-step vs Two Step Diagram

 _

Storage Location-to-Storage Location

 _

Two-step

_

Stock in transit is located in the destination location when checked in stock checking function (MMBE).

To check the stock-in-transit you can use MB5T or MB52 in SAP.

 

Transfer Posting In Different Plants

Transfer Posting Plant-to-Plant

 

Summary

Transfer Posting in SAP S/4HANA facilitates material movements directly in the system. It complements physical stock transfers and is managed in MIGO.

 

Hope it helps,

 

Rezkia

entry.title: Securing Business Partner (BP) Master Data in SAP S/4HANA: A Guide to Field-Level Authorization
entry.content:
Problem Statement

When we have MDG implemented for supplier and customer. All the master data changes for supplier and customer must go through the MDG workflow process and data must not be editable in S/4 system directly, however, there are a few exceptions where specific fields must be managed directly in S4. Due to this we must build our S4 role in such a way that all the MDG managed data / fields must be restricted only to display and only S4 managed fields should be editable.

Implementing The Solution:

Usually in S4 all the business partner data is managed through either transaction BP or the Fiori app F3163 - Manage Business Partner Master Data. The solution we are going to discuss here only works with transaction code BP but not with Fiori apps.

Preparing a requirement matrix:

We should start with identifying which fields / data is MDG managed vs S4 managed. To get this, we should work with the master data team. They can help us with identifying the field level requirements like below with reference to master data dictionary.

 

Technical Filed Name

Business Name

MDG/S4

KNA1.NAME4

Attention (aka Name 4)

S4

KNBK.BANKL

Bank Number

S4

ADRC.STREET

Address Line 1

MDG

KNA1.KDKG1

Customer condition group 1 (Line discount)

S4

KNA1.KDKG2

Customer condition group 2 (Multi line discount)

S4

KNBK.BANKN

Bank Account Number

MDG

KNBK.BANKS

Bank Country Key

MDG

Preparing role matrix:

Now we should prepare a role matrix in line with identified S4 managed fields / data

 

Technical Filed Name

Business Name

Role1

Role2

KNA1.NAME4

Attention (aka Name 4)

X

 

KNBK.BANKL

Bank Number

X

 

KNA1.KDKG1

Customer condition group 1 (Line discount)

 

X

KNA1.KDKG2

Customer condition group 2 (Multi line discount)

 

X

 Identifying the field group ID:

Next step is to Identify the field group IDs, which will be used in authorization object (B_BUPA_FDG) to restrict the access to fields.

 Steps: 

  • Go to SE16 transaction
  • Enter table name “TBZ3R” and execute
  • Filter out using technical filed name

 

 

Marking fields as authorization relevant:

In system not all BP fields are authorizations relevant by default. Hence, first we must make all identified fields (Both MDG and S4 managed fields) as authorization relevant.  

Steps:

  • Go to BUCN transaction
  • Click on “New Entries”
  • Enter the field group IDs identified in earlier
  • Click on save
  • Once you click on save system to prompt to create a transport to capture these changes. We need

to move this transport (along with the role changes) across the landscape to reflect the changes

 

 

Adjusting Role Authorizations:

The first line of security when working with transaction code BP is at BP role type using B_BUPA_RLT authorization object (example: Customer, Supplier etc.). Then comes the field level security restrictions using authorization object B_BUPA_FDG. This is where you will enter the field groups which should be editable for this role.

 

 

Conclusion:

All these configurations and restrictions only work with transaction code BP but not with the Fiori Apps. Hence, only assign transaction code BP to the role and adjust the B_BUPA_RLT object as needed to provide access to required BP role type (example: Customer, Supplier). Then using authorization object B_BUPA_FDG we can restrict the role with display/change access to required fields. We must restrict all the MDG managed fields in S4 roles to display access only otherwise, the role gives access to fields which would bypass any MDG workflows. 

 

Security   SAP HANA   SAP Master Data Governance   SAP S/4HANA business partner

entry.title: SAP MM - INTERCOMPANY (CROSS COMPANY)
entry.content:
I. INTRODUCTION

In SAP MM (Materials Management), Intercompany refers to transactions between different company codes within the same corporate group. These transactions involve the sale, purchase, or transfer of materials between entities that are legally separate but under the same organization.


II. TYPE OF STOCK TRANSFER

 Stock Transport Order without DeliveryStock Transport Order with DeliveryStock Transport Order with Delivery and Billing Document
Order Type MM-PUR

- Order Type UB

- Item Category U

- Order Type UB

- Item Category U

Order Type NB

- Item Category Blank (Cross-Company-Code)

-Item Category U (Intra-Company-Code without Billing Document)

Movement Type MM-IM

- GI: 351

- GR: 101

(One Step Procedure Not Supported)

- GI: 641 (1 Step)

- GI: 647 and GR: 101 (2 Steps)

 

- GI: 643 (1 Step)

- GI: 645 and GR: 101 (2 Steps)

Delivery Type in SD-NLNLCC
Schedule Line Category in SD-NNNC
Billing Type in SD--IV
Document Type MM-IV--RE
PriceValuation Price (Supplying Plant)Valuation Price (Supplying Plant)Pricing in SD and MM
Planning UsingPurchase OrderPurchase OrderPurchase Order
Delivery CostYesYesYes

 

III. PRE-REQUISITE

 

IV. ONE STEP & TWO STEP

1. One Step (Intercompany)

=> Guide: 

SPRO --> Material Management --> Purchasing --> Purchase Order --> Set up STO --> Config. Document Type

Activate One-Step Procedure

Create Purchase Order (ME21N)

Maintain Shipping Data

Check If The Stock is Sufficient

Create Delivery Note (VL10B)

Post Goods Issue (VL02)

You Can Check The Movement Type for One-Step is 645

It Will Automatically Generated the GR for Receiving Plant

Create Billing Document

 

2. Two Step (Intercompany)

==> Guide :

Uncheck The "One Step" To Do The Two Step

Create PO (ME21N)

Check The Material Flow (MMBE)

Create Delivery Note (VL10B), Then Post Goods Issue (VL02)

Check The Stock Is Still In Transit For Two-Step (Need To Post GR Manually)

Post Goods Receipt For The Receiving Plant to Confirm The Stock (MIGO)

Create Billing Document (VF01)

 

V. CONCLUSION

entry.title: Flexible Workflow in SAP S4 HANA Sourcing and Procurement
entry.content:
Spoiler
 

Flexible Workflow for Purchase Contracts and Scheduling Agreements

The flexible workflow is a general function that allows you to define condition-based workflows for approval processes. Approvers can then approve or reject corresponding work items in the app My Inbox. Approvers receive notifications about new work items in the SAP Fiori launchpad. If required, you can also configure e-mail notifications to be sent to them. Requestors can also be notified via e-mail when their purchase contract has been approved or rejected.

Although SAP S4 HANA allows classical Release Strategy to be configured, some of the key differences between Flexible vs Classical workflow is as follows,

 

Functions

Classical Release Strategy

Flexible Workflow

Deadline Monitoring

NA

You can configure automated reminder email if the deadline is crossed for approval

Validity

NA

You can configure validity period for the workflow

Class and Characteristics

You can have only 1 class per purchasing document
and the characteristics are fixed

You can have start and step conditions which does not have to be fixed for each workflow

No. of approvers

Maximum we can have 8 approvers

You can have N no. of approvers

Reviewers

NA

In addition to approvers, we can also set reviewers who can be notified on the approval progress of the purchasing document

Email Notifications

Has to be customised with ABAPERS

Standard templates are available which can be modified. However, advanced customisation is possible only with ABAPERS

HR MM Integration

NA

Here, all approvers should have SAP user ID which has successful BP sync. Use this SAP documentation to set up the sync. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/4cef93946a0b48ec89533b3c34443b85/017b7d3ea4c04710ba802b6bf454c230.html?state=2022

 

Comments

In classical WF, the comments made by approvers is not saved once my inbox is refreshed

All comments made by approvers in purchasing document are saved in the work item.

For Contracts, Workflow ID WS00800304 is and Task ID is TS00800578.

For Scheduling Agreement, Workflow ID WS00800305 is and Task ID is TS00800580.

Customizing Settings in SPRO

1. Go to SWU3 or ABAP Platform >Application Server > Business Management > SAP Business Workflow > Maintain Standard Settings. If you have previously used a workflow functionality (either SAP Business Workflow or flexible workflow), check whether all customizing steps listed have a green checkmark. Else click Redo Automatic Customizing.

2. Activate the workflow scenarios for a type of business document. Go to ABAP Platform > Application Server >   Business Management > SAP Business Workflow  > Flexible Workflow  Scenario Activation. Enter the Workflow ID for Contract and Agreement respectively.

3. Define Step Name and Decision Options. Go to ABAP Platform > SAP Gateway Service Enablement >   Content Workflow Settings > Maintain Task Names and Decision Options. Do not fill the column Icon MIME Repository Path.

4. Define Visualisation Metadata for the My Inbox.

For Contracts,

Go to Materials Management > Purchasing > Contract > Flexible Workflow for Purchase Contracts > Client-Dependent Configuration.

  • As Worklist Client, select SAPUI5 My Inbox or My Inbox
  • Select the Task tab and choose Create New Line.
  • Enter the data listed below.

See the notes at the end for SAP documentation to get this data.

For Scheduling Agreements,

Go to Materials Management > Purchasing > Scheduling Agreement > Flexible Workflow for Purchase Scheduling Agreement > Client-Dependent Configuration.

  • As Worklist Client, select SAPUI5 My Inbox or My Inbox
  • Select the Task tab and choose Create New Line.
  • Enter the data listed below.

See the notes at the end for SAP documentation to get this data.

5. We need to generalise tasks and activate event type linkages. Go to Application Server > Business Management > SAP Business Workflow > Perform task-specific Customizing. Note, you can also remove unwanted previous event type linkages for the bus object.

Navigate to MM > Purchasing > Vendor Outline Agreements > Contracts or Scheduling Agreements

  • Click Assign Agents for Contract or Scheduling Agreement.
  • Select a general task for your business object. (The IDs of tasks start with TS).
  • Click Create Agent Assignment.
  • Select the general tasks.

For Contract,

For Scheduling Agreements,

  • Click Activate Event Linking
  • Select a workflow scenario for your business object. (The IDs of workflow scenarios start with WS).
  • Expand the hierarchy to display the relevant event.
  • Activate the linkage for the listed event.

For Contracts,

For Scheduling Agreements,

6.Register the OData service to enable the value help in the flexible workflow. Go to T Code /n/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE.

  • Choose Add Service.
  • On the Add Selected Services screen, choose System Alias as Local.
  • Add the technical service S_MMPURWORKFLOWVH_CDS and click Get Services.
  • Select the service and click Add Selected Services.

7. Activate Flexible Workflow for specific purchasing document types.

For Contracts,

Material Management > Purchasing > Purchase Contract > Flexible Workflow for Purchase Contract > Activate Flexible Workflow for Contract

For Scheduling Agreements,

Material Management > Purchasing > Purchase Scheduling Agreement > Flexible Workflow for Purchase Scheduling Agreements > Activate Flexible Workflow for Purchase Scheduling Agreements

8. You can also set conditions to restart the flexible workflow.

For Contracts,

Material Management > Purchasing > Purchase Contract > Flexible Workflow for Purchase Contract > Manage Conditions to Restart Flexible Workflow for Contract

For Scheduling Agreements,

Material Management > Purchasing > Purchase Scheduling Agreement > Flexible Workflow for Purchase Scheduling Agreements > Manage Conditions to Flexible Workflow for Purchase Scheduling Agreements

For conditions apart from the above mentioned, you implement BADI.

Configure Flexible Workflow in Fiori

1. Go to Manage Flexible Workflow for Purchase Contracts or Scheduling Agreements App. Click Create.

2. Enter Workflow name, Description, Validity etc.

3.Enter the Start Conditions i.e. the common characteristics to start the flexible workflow. This can be things like company code, document type etc. More specific conditions like target value of purchasing documents can be configured in steps conditions.

4. Click on create workflow steps. Enter Step name, Step type should always be Release of Purchase Document instead of Automatic Release. Here, you can create approvers for the workflow as user or role. You can mention the users in order of approval.

5. You can mention if all recipients should approve for release of purchasing document or only one recipient should release the entire document. Also, you can mention step conditions as described in point 3. The step conditions can be specific such as purchasing group, target value of document etc.

6. You can mark the deadline for the approval. To deadline usually considers all days of the week. If you would like to specify the deadline on the basis of factory calendar, please implement BADI as given in SAP source documentation mentioned in the end of the blog.

7. Under Exception handling, mention the below settings.

8. Click on review steps create button. Similarly mention Review Step Name, Description, Users, Step Conditions, Deadline etc.

9. We should always ensure that our flexible workflow is active, and the order should be 1. The system always reviews the workflows in the order defined. Note, that standard workflow i.e automatic release of purchasing document should always be active and be placed at the end in case of order. This is because, any purchasing document that fails the start conditions of custom workflow will be automatically released with the help of standard workflow.

10. We can customise email templates for task approvers and also send emails when work item is completed or rejected. Emails can also be sent when deadline is crossed.

Go to Maintain Email Templates App.

  • Email notification for recipients of workflow items (approvers)

Copy the pre-delivered e-mail template SWF_CRT_NOTIFY_RECIPIENTS into the custom name space. The custom template must follow this pattern:

<YY1>_<Scenario_Id without WS>_CRT_ALL, or YY1_<Scenario_Id without WS>_CRT_<Node_Id>

For Contracts:  YY1_00800304_ CRT_000009

For Scheduling Agreements:  YY1_00800305_ CRT_000009

  • Email notification for workflow initiators (purchasers) in case of workflow approval:

Copy the pre delivered e-mail template MMPUR_CTR_WFL_APPROVED_EMAIL into your namespace. The custom template must follow this pattern: YY1_<ScenarioId>_COMPLETE_POSITIVE

For Contracts:  YY1_00800304_COMPLETE_POSITIVE

For Scheduling Agreements:  YY1_00800305_COMPLETE_POSITIVE

  • Email notification for workflow initiators (purchasers) in case of workflow rejection:

Copy the pre delivered e-mail template MMPUR_CTR_WFL_REJECTED_EMAIL into your namespace, The custom template must follow this pattern: YY1_<ScenarioId>_COMPLETE_NEGATIVE

For Contracts:  YY1_00800304_COMPLETE_NEGATIVE

For Scheduling Agreements:  YY1_00800305_COMPLETE_ NEGATIVE

  • Email notification for approvers when a purchase contract is pending for approval:

We recommend that you use this template to generate approval action email notifications for purchase contracts that are pending approval for a specified period.

Copy the predelivered email template MMPUR_CTR_WFL_DEADLINE_EMAIL into your namespace. The custom template must follow this pattern: YY1_Name.

 

11. Create the purchasing document via Manage App or GUI as a Tile App. You can see the approval in My Inbox and also can see the email via SOST.

SAP Documentation  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/fdb347ddc69340e89bff5c6dd0d2453d.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/8914920b632d4ff0b4fdea41faccbba6.html

entry.title: Asset Master Data & Asset Purchasing in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Asset

Definition

Assets are resources owned or controlled by an entity that are expected to provide future economic benefits.
In SAP, an asset is typically an object, a right, or an item owned by an enterprise. It is intended for long-term use and can be identified individually in the balance sheet. Each asset belongs to a company code.

Characteristics

  • Ownership or Control: The entity must own or have control over the resource.
  • Future Economic Benefit: The asset is expected to generate cash inflows or reduce cash outflows in the future.
  • Measurable Value: The asset's value can be reliably measured in monetary terms.

Category

Acquire-to-Retire Process

The Acquire-to-Retire process outlines the complete lifecycle of an asset.

 

Asset Lifecycle

The asset lifecycle represents the critical stages of managing an asset in a company.

 

Asset Acquisition

 

Asset Acquisition: External Acquisition

 

Asset Acquisition: External Acquisition: External Vendor

 

SAP Demonstration

Asset Master Data

Create Asset

T-Code: AS01

To create an asset, input the asset name and asset class.

Asset class

  • The asset class is a mandatory field that defines the nature and grouping of the asset (e.g., Vehicles, Buildings, Machinery).
  • It controls the account determination by linking the asset to G/L accounts for acquisition, depreciation, and retirement.
  • Defines default values for depreciation keys, useful life, and other attributes.
  • Correct assignment ensures the financial postings for the asset acquisition flow to the appropriate G/L accounts.

 

Number of similar asset

  • It depicts the number of similar assets that you want the system to create when you save an asset master record.

In the Time-dependent tab, you need to enter the controlling object, either cost center or order related to the asset.

The origin tab is only for statistical.

 

In the depreciation areas tab, fill the depreciation area and the use life of your asset.

LINJ and LINS depicts the depreciation calculation method (straight line method).

After that, click save.

Asset Acquisition

1. Asset Acquisition with Supplier

For single transaction asset purchase.

T-Code: F-90

Scenario:
A company need to purchase 20 vehicles for 1.200.000 CNY and do the financial entry in only one transaction (directly with vendor).

Step 1 - Inputting data for header and credit section.

Here we see that the document type is automatically assigned to KR.

For the posting key, it is autoassigned to 31 (credit for vendor) and credit it to our vendor account payable (1000044).

Step 2 - Credit (Contd.) and Debit Input

Here we enter the credit amount.

For the debit side, we enter posting key 70 for debit asset.

Then input transaction Type 100 for external asset acquisition.

Step 3 - Debit (Contd.)

Next, we enter the debit amount and tax code.

Display Document

T-Code: FB03

This transaction represents an asset acquisition financed via accounts payable.

Debit: Supplier AP
Credit: Asset

  • Line 1 reflects the liability created for the supplier. Upon acquisition of the asset, the company owes the supplier 1,200,000 CNY. This is a typical entry in Accounts Payable, which is a credit account (liability) in financial accounting.
  • Line 2 shows the asset acquisition offset account which is a reconciliation or clearing account that temporarily holds the debit value of the acquisition. This ensures that the accounting structure remains balanced.

2. Asset Acquisition with Offsetting Entry

Acquisition with offsetting entry is to accommodate two transactions for asset receipt and invoice.

T-Code: ABZON

Scenario:
A company need to purchase 100 computers for 500.000 CNY and do the financial entry in two transactions using offsetting entry to separate the asset receipt and invoice receipt.

Section 1 - Asset Receiving Process

Fill out the asset master data. You can create new asset or use your existing asset.

Fill out your asset amount and click save.

Show asset explorer

T-code: AW01N

Display Document

T-Code: FB03

Debit: Asset account
Credit: Clearing account

  • Line 1: This line reflects the debit to asset account for 500,000 CNY. This entry records the receipt of the asset due to the asset acquisition.
  • Line 2: This line shows the credit to technical clearing account for 500,000 CNY. This clearing account acts as a temporary holding account to balance the asset acquisition.

Section 2 - Vendor Invoice Posting

Display Document

T-Code: FB03

  • Line 1: This line reflects the payment made to the vendor, resulting in a credit to the vendor's account (Accounts Payable) for 500,000 CNY.
  • Line 2: This line shows the debit to the technical clearing account for 500,000 CNY. This clearing account acts as a temporary holding account to balance the payment transaction.

3. Asset Acquisition with Materials Management

Workflow:

Scenario:
A company need to purchase 1 plumbing fixtures for 1.000 CNY. This is a one-time purchase and company need the financial entry integration with MM. When the invoice comes, apparently the price is 1.002 CNY.

a. Without GR Valuation

Step 1 - Create Purchase Order

T-Code: ME21N

 

Fields to enter:

Header:

  • Purchasing Organizations
  • Purchasing Group
  • Company Code

Item Overview

  • Account Assignment: A (Asset)
  • Short text
  • PO Quantity: 1
  • Order Unit: PC
  • Delivery Date
  • Net Price: 1000
  • Material Group: YBFA06
  • Plant
  • Storage Location

Item Detail:

  • Asset master record
  • GR non valuated.

Step 2 - Goods Receipt

T-Code  : MIGO
Movement Type  : 101

After confirming the Goods Receipt, we can not see the finance documents here.

It is because we mark the GR non valuated in the PO.

Step 3 - Invoice Receipt

T-Code: MIRO

Suppose that the price is 1.002 CNY so there is a 2 CNY gap. With tax, the total invoice is 1.132,26 CNY.

Here we can see that there are 3 accounting documents here. 2 of them depicts the different version of ledger (0L and 2L) for the asset receiving process, meanwhile the other one shows the debit for the vendor account.

Here are the financial documents:

Ledger Group 0L

 

Ledger Group 2L

 

Credit to Vendor Account

  • The transaction in Ledger Groups 0L (GAAP) and 2L (IFRS) is identical, excluding tax calculations, demonstrating that the accounting standards do not impact the transaction in this specific instance.
  • For 1&2, the debit is in the asset account while the credit is on the technical clearing account which act as an intermediary account. Since we do not use the GR valuation, so we only have the financial entry at the invoice.
  • For 3, the clearing account is debited, then credited against the vendor account, effectively settling the invoice.

b. With GR Valuation

The difference with the previous scenario is we put the valuation of our asset when doing the goods receipt.

Step 1 - Create Purchase Order

T-Code: ME21N

In this scenario, we will not tick the GR non valuated option.

Step 2 - Good Receipt

T-code: MIGO
Movement Type: 101

After confirming the Goods Receipt, we can see the finance documents.

Here we see the asset accounting document with the amount posted is 1.000 CNY on 12-12-2024 and transaction type 120 for good receipt.

Financial documents for Goods Receipt

 

  • In the 1, it shows a debit to technical asset acquisition clearing account and a credit to GR/IR.
  • Meanwhile in the 2 and 3, it shows a debit to the fixed asset account and a credit to the technical asset acquisition clearing account.
  • The amount reflects the amount in the purchase order.

 

 

Step 3 - Invoice Receipt

T-code: MIRO

Suppose that the price is 1.002 CNY so there is a 2 CNY gap. With tax, the total invoice is 1.132,26 CNY.

Financial document data

1.Ledger Groups (0L & 2L): Same transaction, no tax calculation (unlike blank ledger group).

2.Entries for 1 & 2:

  • Debit: Fixed Asset Account.
  • Credit: Technical Clearing Account (intermediary for price differences).
  • Note: No price difference = No financial entry.

3.Entry for 3:

  • Credit: Supplier AP (1,132.26 CNY).
  • Debit: GR/IR Account, price difference, and tax.
  • Result: Balanced transaction.

 

 

That's all for asset management ❤️

Hope it helps!

 

Rezkia

entry.title: Product Compliance (new): automatic substance checks not starting with mass uploaded materials
entry.content:
Dear Community

Issue with SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance (on-prem, release 2023), Product Marketability and Chemical Compliance solution capability.

Solution went live a month ago, with the first bundle of products/materials views (Raw and Unpacked) being created "by hand". 

As this process is very time consuming we are mass uploading materials data via Migration Cockpit. All is well with a major exception: the automatic substance checks, from the compliance requirements such as EU REACH SVHC Status, are not starting and thus, the compliance assessments are not performed.

I'm uploading the data with this sequence:

1. Bulk Raw Material Compliance Info -> 2. (related) Packaged Raw Material Compliance Info -> 3. (Bulk) Product Compliance Info -> 4. (related) Packaged Product Compliance Info (if necessary*) - 5. Analytical Composition 

* When uploading the Raw Material Compliance Info, if the logistic role of "sold" is marked, the Unpacked Compliance info is created.

For the time being we are letting out the materials that (will) have a material based composition (as these are few with my client).

Once again, I kindly ask for your advice and help

 

Paulo Pinto

entry.title: An in-depth look at clean core business processes, read our Clean Core for S/4 HANA Cloud paper!
entry.content:
Have you ever wondered where to start to understand more about the benefits of a clean core for your enterprise system and how to achieve one? If you are already on a clean core journey do you want tips and tricks to get more value out of it?

Then our new paper on Clean Core business processes for SAP S/4HANA Cloud is for you! It brings you in-depth insights into the importance of standardization of business processes in getting started with clean core achievement for your enterprise systems.

Our aim is to provide you with a set of guiding principles for building resilient business processes based on industry standards and justify differentiating extensions, supported by seamless integration, reliable data quality and efficient operations. This is laid out in the four chapters of the white paper which are as follows:

  • Introduction to clean core strategy
  • Importance of business processes in the SAP clean core strategy
  • Fit to Standard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Getting started with Fit to Standard.

 

What is the importance of business processes in SAP’s “clean core” strategy?

A clean digital core provides flexibility and enables scale for changing business requirements, is easier to maintain and upgrade, and has a stable design on which latest solution capabilities and innovations can be adopted with relative ease.

The clean core concept has multiple principles, namely: Business Processes, Extensibility, Data, Integration and Operations.

In this blog post, we will focus on the principle of business processes and the importance of reducing complexity by increasing adherence to industry standards as a starting point for transformations. We shall also present the importance of treating business process management as a long term organizational best practice, the process to be followed when standard processes do not fulfil business value objectives, as well as the integrated tool chain recommendations for managing business processes.

 

How process standardisation supports “clean core” achievement in transformations

Today’s transformation methodologies need to ensure that business process management (BPM) is a continuous organizational practice that goes hand in hand with system transformations, as well as continuous improvements to, and regular operations of, those systems.

A good BPM practice delivers:

 

SAP Activate, therefore, integrates BPM throughout its phases to ensure standardization and best use of the capabilities and innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This is driven by the Fit to Standard (F2S) mindset deeply embedded in SAP Activate.

In chapter 2 of this white paper, we provide detailed guidance on the role of process standardization in modern transformations across all phases of the SAP Activate methodology.

 

How to achieve process standardization – Fit to Standard

Fit to Standard is a fundamental new way of thinking in the cloud era. It provides the ability to rapidly adopt standard best practices and leverage cloud technology to establish a foundation for engaging in continuous innovation. A well-defined fit to standard approach enables an organization to achieve higher agility, flexibility and collaboration, thereby optimizing the use of business and technology innovations that the cloud offers.

The RISE with SAP Methodology provides clear guidance on how clean core adherence should be driven by the fit to standard approach, and the role that proper governance (through a well-functioning solution standardization board) plays in transformations. More details on fit to standard approach and accelerators, requirement and gap management, as well as, the solution standardization board, can be found in chapter 3 of the white paper. SAP also provides maturity assessment and jumpstart services for solution standardization board in your program through Design and Value Governance service.

 

How to Get Started?

First and foremost, read our white paper on Clean Core business processes for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The last chapter of the document details the four-step path to getting started with a fit to standard approach:

  1. Focus on mindset and adopt the right methodology and tools
  2. Nail down the scope and identify the standards available
  3. Identify value creation opportunities through process standardization and innovation adoption
  4. Establish solution standardization board to govern deviations from standard and ensure that every gap extension is justified appropriately and linked to value creation opportunities.

Chapter 4 of this white paper also provides detailed information and links to understand the role that the following tools in the integrated tool chain plays in transformation execution as prescribed in the RISE with SAP Methodology:

  • SAP LeanIX
  • SAP Signavio
  • SAP CALM

Now you're ready to begin your fruitful journey to achieve Clean Core by leveraging Clean Core business processes for SAP S/4HANA Cloud ensure that you have maximized the adoption of standard industry processes and focussed your energies on achieving a clean extensibility in those processes where you can truly differentiate from your peers and competitors. For more details on continuing your journey to clean core, you should also refer to the Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud white paper.
